title: CLAUDE.md: terse rules vs verbose prose
description: Does a long, polite CLAUDE.md beat a short, imperative one? Same tasks, same model, measured.

tasks.prompt: The function `lastChunk(arr, size)` in chunk.js should return the final chunk of `arr` of length `size` (or the remainder if shorter). It has a bug. Fix it so the tests pass. Run the tests with `node --test`.
Implement `parseRange(header)` in range.js. It parses an HTTP Range header like `bytes=0-499` and returns `{ start, end }` as numbers, or `null` if malformed. Make the tests pass with `node --test`.
`dedupe(items)` in dedupe.js works but is O(n^2) and mutates its input. Refactor it to be O(n) and pure, preserving first-seen order. Keep all tests passing via `node --test`.
